answers.value: McKinley Park Playground Accessibility and Safety Upgrade Project
answers.reasoning: Pre-filled value from ground truth form

answers.value: McKinley Park, 601 Alhambra Blvd, Sacramento, CA 95816; APNs 007-0223-004 and 007-0223-005
answers.reasoning: Pre-filled value from ground truth form
Pre-filled value from ground truth form
Pre-filled value from ground truth form

answers.value: The McKinley Park Playground Accessibility and Safety Upgrade Project consists of the removal and replacement of approximately 12,500 square feet of existing, aging play equipment and surfacing within the existing McKinley Park playground area, located in the central portion of the park adjacent to the existing community center. The project will: (1) remove deteriorated play structures, swings, and associated footings; (2) remove existing non-compliant wood fiber surfacing and selected sections of adjacent concrete that do not meet current ADA slope and cross-slope requirements; (3) install new, ADA-compliant poured-in-place rubberized safety surfacing; (4) install new, modern play equipment designed for children ages 2–5 and 5–12, including inclusive play elements such as transfer platforms, ground-level sensory panels, and accessible swings; (5) upgrade and reconfigure existing accessible pathways connecting the playground to the adjacent parking lot, restrooms, and community center to meet current California Building Code and ADA standards; (6) install new perimeter fencing and safety signage; and (7) provide minor associated site work, including replacement of several small sections of turf and landscaping disturbed during construction. No expansion of the existing playground footprint is proposed, no increase in overall park capacity would occur, and all work would be confined to the already-developed and intensively used recreational area of the park. Construction activities would occur during normal daytime hours, use standard light-duty construction equipment, and are anticipated to last approximately four months.

answers.field_label: Categorical Exemption State Type And Section Number
answers.value: Class 1 (Existing Facilities), Section 15301; Class 3 (New Construction or Conversion of Small Structures), Section 15303; and Class 4 (Minor Alterations to Land), Section 15304
answers.reasoning: Pre-filled value from ground truth form

answers.value:
The project qualifies for a categorical exemption under the California Environmental Quality Act (CEQA) pursuant to Sections 15301 (Existing Facilities), 15303 (New Construction or Conversion of Small Structures), and 15304 (Minor Alterations to Land) of the State CEQA Guidelines.

Section 15301 (Existing Facilities) applies because the project involves minor alterations to an existing public recreational facility (McKinley Park playground) that is already developed, heavily used by the public, and served by existing utilities and infrastructure. The proposed work consists primarily of replacement and upgrading of existing play equipment, surfacing, and associated access paths within the same general footprint as the current playground. There will be no increase in the overall size of the playground area, no increase in parking supply, and no intensification of use beyond what currently occurs at the site. The primary purpose of the project is to address deferred maintenance, correct safety hazards, and bring the facility into compliance with current safety and accessibility standards, which are typical examples of activities covered by Class 1.

Section 15303 (New Construction or Conversion of Small Structures) applies because the new play equipment and associated site furnishings constitute small accessory structures within an existing urban park setting. The project includes installation of new prefabricated play structures, swings, benches, and shade elements of a scale and type commonly found in neighborhood and community parks. These features are relatively small in size, are accessory to the existing park use, and do not involve substantial grading, building pads, or new building construction. The project does not add any enclosed buildings, restrooms, or other major facilities; instead, it replaces and slightly reconfigures existing small-scale recreational amenities.

Section 15304 (Minor Alterations to Land) applies because ground disturbance will be limited to minor grading, trenching, and surface preparation necessary to remove old surfacing and install new rubberized surfacing, foundations for play equipment, and short segments of accessible pathways. All work will occur within an already landscaped and developed park area. Disturbance will be confined to previously disturbed turf and planting areas and will not involve the removal of healthy, mature, or scenic trees. Any small shrubs or turf removed will be replaced with similar landscaping following construction. There will be no expansion of the existing park boundaries, no changes to drainage patterns beyond negligible localized adjustments within the playground area, and no work in or near waterways, wetlands, or other sensitive biological resources.

The project site is located in a fully urbanized area of the City of Sacramento, surrounded by existing residential neighborhoods and public streets. Utilities (water, sewer, storm drain, and electricity) are already in place serving the park, and no new utility corridors or off-site improvements are required. Construction traffic will be limited to a short-term increase in trips for delivery of materials and equipment, which will be minor compared to the existing traffic on adjacent streets and will cease upon completion of construction. Noise generated during construction will be temporary, will occur only during permitted daytime hours consistent with the City’s noise ordinance, and will be comparable to typical park maintenance and small construction projects in urban areas.

There are no unusual circumstances that would result in a reasonable possibility of a significant effect on the environment. The project does not involve work in or near a designated habitat for any threatened or endangered species, nor does it affect any known cultural or historical resource. McKinley Park is a long-established urban park, and the playground area in particular has been disturbed repeatedly over decades of installation, removal, and replacement of recreational equipment. The project will not result in substantial changes in population, traffic patterns, air quality emissions, greenhouse gas emissions, or public services. Rather, it will provide safer and more accessible facilities for existing users of the park, including children with disabilities and their caregivers.

Furthermore, none of the exceptions to the use of categorical exemptions set forth in CEQA Guidelines Section 15300.2 apply. The project is not located in a particularly sensitive environmental resource area, does not contribute to a cumulative impact when viewed in connection with past, current, or probable future projects, does not result in a significant effect due to unusual circumstances, does not affect scenic resources within a designated state scenic highway, is not located on a site included on a list of hazardous materials sites compiled pursuant to Government Code Section 65962.5, and does not cause substantial adverse change in the significance of a historical resource. For these reasons, the project is appropriately found to be exempt from CEQA under the cited categorical exemptions.
